user_query:
I’m preparing a continuity brief for three ongoing projects based on our past chats across sessions. Please mine my historical conversations to capture what we’ve already tried, what worked, what failed, open issues, and my preferences. Read input/projects.csv for the list of project codenames and seed topics.

What to do per project:
- Generate 2 short, focused memory search queries (<= 8 words each) derived from the codename and topics.
- Include at least one query per project that explicitly filters for my past statements (use role='user' in the query string when exploring preferences).
- If a hit references a specific task identifier, expand with a full narrative summary; if you need more context around a single mention, plan a short timeline expansion around that message.
- If you cannot find reliable details, mark the relevant fields as "unknown" and log a refined follow-up query.

Deliverables:
1) Write output/brief.json — a structured continuity brief with:
   - generated_at (ISO-ish timestamp string)
   - projects: an array of 3 objects (one per codename), each containing:
     - project_code
     - queries (the 2 short queries you used)
     - memory_found (true/false)
     - task_summaries (array of objects, may be empty)
     - timeline_notes (array of objects, may be empty)
     - preferences (array, may be empty)
     - what_worked (string)
     - what_failed (string)
     - open_issues (array with at least 1 item)
     - next_steps (array with at least 1 item)
   Use "unknown" for any detail you could not confirm.
2) Write output/search_log.md — a chronological log of your search attempts and context expansions. Create clear sections for each project (e.g., "## phoenix", etc.) and use these tags in-line so it’s easy to skim: [SEARCH] for each query attempted, [SUMMARY] when you assemble a full narrative summary from a prior task, and [TIMELINE] when you expand context around a single mention. For each project, log both queries and any follow-up refined query when details were unknown.
3) Write output/assumptions.txt — list any assumptions you made because the memory lacked specifics (one bullet per line starting with "- ").

Notes:
- Keep queries short and concrete, derived from the codename and topics in input/projects.csv.
- Use role='user' in at least one query per project to focus on my expressed preferences.
- Do not include any personal data beyond what you actually infer from prior context; if unsure, write "unknown".
- Make sure all output files are placed under output/ with the exact filenames above.
